sections.content:
The SPACE action caused a significant change. Let me compare before and after:
Before SPACE:
- Node 13: White rectangle at rows 34-43, cols 27-36 (100 pixels)
- Node 12: Purple rectangle at rows 25-31, cols 26-37 (84 pixels)
After SPACE:
- Node 13: Purple rectangle at rows 34-38, cols 27-36 (50 pixels) 
- Node 14: White rectangle at rows 39-43, cols 27-36 (50 pixels)
- Node 12: Still purple at rows 25-31, cols 26-37 (84 pixels)
So the white rectangle (100 pixels, 10x10) was split into a purple top half (5x10) and white bottom half (5x10). The animation had 15 frames with 208 cells changed.
Looking at the top-left panel as a reference: white on top, purple on bottom. The main area now has purple on top of white for the lower block, which matches the pattern from the top-left panel!
Let me check the animation to understand what happened better, and see if the level is complete now or if there's more to do.

print(f"last_action_result: {last_action_result}")

# Check current state vs before

for n in seg['nodes']:

        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if level is done
print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")
print(f"Score: {last_action_result.get('score', 0)}")

# Let me see the current state of the main area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Main area rows 24-44 ===")
for i in range(24, 45):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][20:45]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
